questions.q: What kind of being was Hidimbi?
options: An apsara of Indra's court
A rakshasi of the forest
A naga princess
A yakshini of the mountains
questions.explanation: Hidimbi was a rakshasi, a demoness of the forest, who could change her shape and travel at will.

questions.q: Who was Hidimbi's brother?
options: Hidimba
Bakasura
Kirmira
Alambusha
questions.explanation: Her brother was the man-eating rakshasa Hidimba, lord of the forest where the Pandavas took shelter.

questions.q: With which Pandava did Hidimbi fall in love?
options: Arjuna
Yudhishthira
Bhima
Nakula
questions.explanation: Sent to kill the sleeping travelers, she was instead captivated by the mighty Bhima standing guard.

questions.q: What task had Hidimbi's brother given her when he sent her toward the sleeping Pandavas?
options: To lure them into a swamp
To steal their weapons while they slept
To learn who they were and report back
To kill them and bring them to him as food
questions.explanation: Hidimba smelled the humans and sent his sister to slay them and fetch their flesh for his meal.

questions.q: Who was the son of Hidimbi and Bhima?
options: Ghatotkacha
Barbarika
Iravan
Anjanaparvan
questions.explanation: Their son Ghatotkacha, bald as a pot at birth, would later die a hero for the Pandavas at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Who killed Hidimbi's brother in the forest?
options: Arjuna
Bhima
Yudhishthira
Kunti's guards
questions.explanation: Bhima fought the enraged rakshasa hand to hand and killed him while the rest of the family looked on.

questions.q: At what point in the Pandavas' story did they encounter Hidimbi?
options: During the thirteenth year of disguise
Just before Draupadi's swayamvara at Kampilya
While fleeing through the forest after the house of lac
During the building of Indraprastha
questions.explanation: The exhausted fugitives were sleeping in Hidimba's forest soon after escaping the burning lac palace at Varanavata.

questions.q: What form did Hidimbi take when she approached Bhima?
options: That of a beautiful human woman
That of an old forest hermit
That of a golden deer
That of a wandering minstrel
questions.explanation: Using her rakshasi powers she assumed a lovely human form to speak with the man she had fallen in love with.

questions.q: Whose consent did Hidimbi seek for her marriage to Bhima?
options: King Drupada's
Vyasa's
Her brother's
Kunti's
questions.explanation: She appealed to Kunti, Bhima's mother, who consented while Yudhishthira added the conditions of the union.

questions.q: What was Bhima doing when Hidimbi first saw him in the forest?
options: Cooking a meal over a fire
Keeping watch while his mother and brothers slept
Bathing in the river at dawn
Gathering fruit for the family
questions.explanation: Bhima alone stayed awake guarding Kunti and his sleeping brothers, and the watching rakshasi lost her heart to him.

questions.q: Why did Hidimba's plan to eat the Pandavas fail?
options: The Pandavas had set a ring of fires around their camp
Vyasa arrived and shielded them with a spell
His sister fell in love with Bhima and warned him instead
The sunrise robbed the rakshasa of his strength
questions.explanation: Hidimbi betrayed her brother's errand, telling Bhima of the danger and offering to carry the family to safety.

questions.q: Under the terms of the marriage, what was Hidimbi required to do at the end of each day?
options: Bring Bhima back to his mother and brothers by nightfall
Return to her brother's old dwelling alone
Offer prayers for the souls of her slain kin
Hunt and provide food for the whole family
questions.explanation: She could roam with Bhima wherever she pleased by day, but had to return him to his family every evening.

questions.q: How long was the marriage of Bhima and Hidimbi agreed to last?
options: Twelve years and a day
Until the Pandavas reclaimed their kingdom
Until Kunti released them from the bond
Until Hidimbi bore a son
questions.explanation: The union was to last until a son was born, after which Bhima would be free to continue on with his brothers.

questions.q: Who stopped Bhima when he moved to kill Hidimbi after her brother's death?
options: Kunti
Yudhishthira
Arjuna
Sahadeva
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira forbade it, declaring that slaying a woman who posed no threat was against dharma.

questions.q: How did Hidimba react when he found his sister in human form beside the Pandavas?
options: He raged at her betrayal and threatened to kill her along with them
He laughed and offered Bhima a wager
He fled to gather other rakshasas
He begged the Pandavas to leave his forest
questions.explanation: Furious at her treachery, Hidimba rushed to kill his sister first, and Bhima stepped in to fight him instead.

questions.q: What did Bhima do so that the duel with Hidimba would not disturb his sleeping family?
options: He asked the demon to wait until morning
He blindfolded the demon with a cloth
He dragged the rakshasa a long way off before fighting
He fought in total silence with wrestling holds alone
questions.explanation: Bhima hauled the demon a good distance away, said to be thirty-two cubits, so the noise would not wake Kunti and his brothers.

questions.q: What help did Hidimbi offer the Pandavas when she warned them of her brother?
options: A hidden store of food in the forest
Knowledge of secret mantras of protection
A map of paths leading out of the forest
To carry them through the air to safety
questions.explanation: With a rakshasi's power of flight she offered to bear them away beyond her brother's reach.

questions.q: Why did Arjuna urge Bhima to finish the fight with Hidimba quickly?
options: Because the family had to reach Ekachakra by dawn
Because rakshasas grow stronger at twilight when darkness aids their wiles
Because the noise was waking the whole forest
Because Kunti had fainted at the sight of the demon
questions.explanation: Arjuna warned that the twilight hour swells rakshasa strength and illusion, so the demon had to die before it arrived.

questions.q: How does the epic explain Ghatotkacha's astonishingly swift arrival into the world?
options: Rakshasis give birth the very day they conceive, and the boy grew up at once
Shiva blessed the unborn child to skip childhood
Hidimbi carried him for ten years before his birth
Vyasa hastened the birth with a mantra
questions.explanation: The epic says rakshasa women bring forth on the day of conception, and Ghatotkacha grew to youth immediately.

questions.q: What did Hidimbi do after her son was born, in keeping with the marriage agreement?
options: She followed the Pandavas in secret for years
She returned alone to her dead brother's lair
She took her leave with the boy and let the Pandavas continue their road
She entered the fire to join her slain kin
questions.explanation: True to her word, she departed with Ghatotkacha, who promised to come to his father whenever he was needed.

questions.q: On what grounds did Hidimbi plead with Kunti to be accepted as Bhima's wife?
options: She promised to win back the Pandavas' kingdom
She claimed the marriage had been fated by Shiva
She offered her forest wealth as dowry
She had forsaken her kin for love and begged not to be abandoned
questions.explanation: She told Kunti she had given up everything for love of Bhima and pleaded that abandoning her would destroy her.

questions.q: How did Bhima and Hidimbi spend the days of their marriage?
options: Ruling the rakshasas of the forest from her brother's seat
She carried him to beautiful mountains and groves, returning him each evening
They wandered as pilgrims among the shrines of the north
They stayed hidden in a cave guarded by her kin
questions.explanation: Taking lovely forms, she bore Bhima through scenic heights and forests by day and faithfully returned him at nightfall.

questions.q: How is Hidimbi actually named in Vyasa's Sanskrit text?
options: Hidimba, the same name as her brother in its feminine form
Kamini, the loving one
Vanadevi, goddess of the wood
Ghatotkachi, mother of Ghatotkacha
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit calls both siblings Hidimba, the sister distinguished only by the feminine ending, while Hidimbi is the popular later form.

questions.q: Where is Hidimbi worshipped as a goddess to this day?
options: At a shrine in Kurukshetra, Haryana
On the banks of the Ganga at Haridwar
At the Hidimba Devi temple in Manali, Himachal Pradesh
In a cave temple at Badami, Karnataka
questions.explanation: The pagoda-style Hidimba Devi temple at Manali, built in 1553, still honors her as a local goddess.

questions.q: In later tradition, which famous warrior was Hidimbi's grandson?
options: Iravan, sacrificed before the war
Barbarika, the three-arrow warrior
Vrishaketu, the last son of Karna
Abhimanyu, the breaker of the wheel
questions.explanation: Barbarika, Ghatotkacha's son who is worshipped in folk tradition as Khatu Shyam, makes Hidimbi a grandmother in texts like the Skanda Purana.

questions.q: What powers made Hidimbi able to guide and carry the Pandavas through the wild?
options: A boon of strength granted by Kubera
Command over the beasts of the forest
Knowledge of every star and season
The rakshasa gifts of changing form and moving through the air at will
questions.explanation: Like all her kind she could assume any shape and travel where she wished, powers she used in service of her new family.

questions.q: What name does tradition give the forest where the Pandavas slept and met the rakshasa siblings?
options: Hidimbavana, the wood of Hidimba
Kamyakavana, the wood of desire
Dvaitavana, the wood of the twin lakes
Naimisharanya, the wood of the sages
questions.explanation: The forest is remembered as Hidimbavana after its rakshasa lord, while Kamyaka and Dvaita belong to the later exile.

questions.q: How did Bhima finally kill the rakshasa Hidimba?
options: He pinned him beneath a boulder until dawn
He throttled him in the coils of a vine
He whirled him in the air and dashed him to the ground, breaking his body
He drove the demon onto the spears of his brothers
questions.explanation: Bhima lifted and whirled the exhausted demon, then smashed him down and broke him, ending the duel before twilight.

questions.q: Which sub-book of the Adi Parva narrates the meeting with Hidimbi and the death of her brother?
options: Jatugriha Parva
Hidimba-vadha Parva
Baka-vadha Parva
Chaitraratha Parva
questions.explanation: The Hidimba-vadha Parva, the section on the slaying of Hidimba, sits between the lac house episode and the slaying of Baka.

questions.q: Who pronounced the condition that Bhima must be returned to his family every nightfall?
options: Kunti, as the price of her blessing
Vyasa, foreseeing the son to come
Bhima himself, unwilling to leave his brothers
Yudhishthira, granting the marriage on that daily term
questions.explanation: In Vyasa's text Yudhishthira voices the nightly condition while Kunti gives her consent, which is why retellings credit the terms to both.
